elements.title: Te Ahi Tupua
elements.contents: ... that Te Ahi Tupua (pictured) is reportedly the largest 3D-printed sculpture in the world?

elements.title: Babe LeVoir
elements.contents: ... that Minnesota's national-championship quarterback Babe LeVoir became the commander of US aircraft-carrier athletics in the Pacific during World War II?

elements.title: Clinton plan
elements.contents: ... that a purported "Clinton plan" cited by Trump officials came from Russian intelligence material later described as likely disinformation?

elements.title: Duetos
elements.contents: ... that the album Duetos, despite receiving mixed reviews, sold more than 100,000 copies within three weeks of release?

elements.title: Jade Jones
elements.contents: ... that Jade Jones, Great Britain's first Olympic gold medallist in taekwondo, now competes as a boxer?

elements.title: a Justin Timberlake song
elements.contents: ... that a reviewer called a Justin Timberlake song a "clunky jungle-as-sex metaphor"?

elements.title: Alice in Wonderland
elements.contents: ... that designs for the 1932 Broadway production of Alice in Wonderland mirrored the original novel's illustrations "down to the smallest prop"?

elements.title: Castelluccio di Norcia
elements.contents: ... that winter snows were so deep in Castelluccio di Norcia that residents dug tunnels to reach the church?

elements.title: a "banana woman"
elements.contents: ... that a "banana woman" opposed a "banana miracle"?
